# 2026-05-08

woke up thinking about the [BetaWidgets margin trend](../companies/betawidgets-inc.md#margin-trend) again. it doesn't add up. gross margin compressed 380bps yoy but they explicitly called out *positive* mix shift on the Q1 call. one of these is wrong.

actually — could be that the mix shift is real but smaller than COGS inflation. need to back-solve. todo.

## morning

read through [BetaWidgets Q1 10-Q](../external/betawidgets-10q-2026q1.pdf) (the [annotated copy](../external/sector-report-q1-2026.html#betawidgets) — see the sector report's bw section). things that stood out:

- raw materials line +14% yoy — they didn't lock-in like ACME did. see [[supply-contracts-note]]
- inventory days up to 71 from 58. that's a lot
- they bought back $42M of stock in Q1. with leverage at 3.4x? bold

> [!warning] sanity check: ACME's own inventory days = 49. BW used to run lower than us. that's a real reversal.

[[Dana Liu]] thinks the inventory build is intentional — pre-positioning ahead of the tariff window. plausible. [#counterparty-risk](#counterparty-risk) #due-diligence

## afternoon

prep for the [portfolio review](../meetings/2026-05-07-portfolio-review.md) follow-up — see [Yuki Tanaka](../people/yuki-tanaka.md)'s slide deck request. she wants the [comparable set decision](../decisions/2026-q2-thesis-call.md#comparable-set) signed off before friday.

also: re-read [[DCF framework]] section on terminal value sensitivity. our exit multiple assumption (12x EBITDA) looks aggressive vs current trading comps at ~9.5x. see [models/dcf-framework.md#terminal-value](../methodology/dcf-framework.md#terminal-value).

## evening — quick thoughts

if i'm right that BW's margin story is mix + supply chain (not pricing), then their re-rating depends entirely on the tariff resolution. and that's a coin flip. would not buy here. would short into earnings if i ran a long-short book. (i don't. mostly.)

probably wrong on the magnitude but directionally right. confidence: medium-low.

## counterparty risk

separately — [Tariq Osei](../people/tariq-osei.md) flagged that BW's top customer (unnamed, ~22% of revenue) renewed at materially worse terms. unconfirmed. need to pull the [external sector report](../external/sector-report-q1-2026.html) and check appendix B.
